question: [File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation / USDSToken.returnFromPool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (liquidator) under PRECONDITIONS (N troves opened when USDSGasCompensation=10e18, then SystemState.setUSDSGasCompensation raised to 20e18 via TimeLock before liquidation) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (batchLiquidateTroves([trove1, trove2, ...]) → _addLiquidationValuesToTotals accumulates totalUSDSGasCompensation = N * 20e18 → _sendGasCompensation calls usdsToken.returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, liquidator, N*20e18)), violating INVARIANT (GasPool USDS balance must equal sum of per-trove gas compensation actually deposited at open time, not live parameter value), causing scoped impact: liquidator receives more USDS from GasPool than was ever deposited for those troves, draining GasPool below zero or stealing from other troves' reserves? Local proof idea: add unit test deploying system with USDSGasCompensation=10e18, open 3 troves (GasPool receives 30e18), raise parameter to 20e18, batch-liquidate all 3, assert liquidator receives 60e18 and GasPool balance goes negative (revert) or assert GasPool balance after < 0 expected.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: TroveManager._sendGasCompensation / USDSToken.returnFromPool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (liquidator) under PRECONDITIONS (USDSGasCompensation decreased from 10e18 to 5e18 via TimeLock after troves opened, GasPool holds 10e18 per trove) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (liquidate(trove) → _liquidateNormalMode sets singleLiquidation.USDSGasCompensation = systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() = 5e18 → _sendGasCompensation transfers only 5e18 from GasPool to liquidator), violating INVARIANT (GasPool balance must be fully drained per closed trove, no residual USDS should remain stranded), causing scoped impact: 5e18 USDS per liquidated trove is permanently stranded in GasPool with no extraction path, creating a growing accounting surplus that cannot be recovered? Local proof idea: add unit test open trove (GasPool=10e18), lower USDSGasCompensation to 5e18, liquidate, assert GasPool.balance == 5e18 (stranded) and no function exists to recover it.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: BorrowerOperations.closeTrove / USDSToken.burn] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (borrower) under PRECONDITIONS (trove opened with USDSGasCompensation=10e18, TimeLock raises USDSGasCompensation to 20e18 before close, GasPool holds only 10e18) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (closeTrove() → reads USDS_GAS_COMPENSATION = systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() = 20e18 → _repayUSDS(activePool, usdsToken, gasPoolAddress, 20e18) → usdsToken.burn(gasPoolAddress, 20e18) → SafeMath underflow revert), violating INVARIANT (closeTrove must always succeed for a solvent borrower who holds sufficient USDS), causing scoped impact: borrower is permanently unable to close their trove and recover collateral because GasPool has insufficient balance for the new higher gas compensation value? Local proof idea: unit test open trove (GasPool=10e18), raise USDSGasCompensation to 20e18, attempt closeTrove, assert revert due to GasPool balance underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: TroveManager._redeemCloseTrove / USDSToken.burn] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (redeemer) under PRECONDITIONS (trove opened with USDSGasCompensation=10e18, TimeLock raises USDSGasCompensation to 20e18 before redemption, GasPool holds 10e18) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (redeemCollateral() → _redeemCollateralFromTrove reads v.usdsGasCompensation = systemState.getUSDSGasCompensation() = 20e18 → full redemption path → _redeemCloseTrove calls usdsToken.burn(gasPoolAddress, 20e18) → SafeMath underflow revert), violating INVARIANT (full redemption must always succeed when redeemer holds sufficient USDS), causing scoped impact: redeemer cannot fully redeem a trove because GasPool has insufficient balance for the inflated gas compensation, blocking the redemption mechanism? [File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: USDSToken._requireValidRecipient / USDSToken.transfer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (any USDS holder) under PRECONDITIONS (attacker holds USDS, GasPool address is not in _requireValidRecipient blocklist which only blocks stabilityPoolAddress, troveManagerAddress, borrowerOperationsAddress) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (usdsToken.transfer(gasPoolAddress, X) succeeds because GasPool is not a blocked recipient → GasPool USDS balance inflated by X), violating INVARIANT (GasPool USDS balance must equal exactly count(active_troves) * USDSGasCompensation), causing scoped impact: GasPool balance exceeds protocol liabilities; when all troves are closed or liquidated, excess USDS is permanently stranded in GasPool with no recovery path, representing a fund loss? [File: 'File Name: src/GasPool.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: USDSToken.returnFromPool / USDSToken._requireCallerIsTroveMorSP] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (StabilityPool is an authorized caller of returnFromPool per _requireCallerIsTroveMorSP, StabilityPool has any code path that calls usdsToken.returnFromPool with _poolAddress=gasPoolAddress) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (attacker triggers StabilityPool function → StabilityPool calls usdsToken.returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, attacker, amount) → USDS transferred from GasPool to attacker), violating INVARIANT (GasPool USDS must only be transferred via TroveManager liquidation/redemption paths or BorrowerOperations close), causing scoped impact: unauthorized extraction of USDS from GasPool via StabilityPool's returnFromPool authorization?
